steps.title: Identify the defining location contrast
steps.content: The study examined two PAs in starkly different settings: Lower Zambezi is remote with few alternative attractions, while Abrolhos is near additional amenities outside the PA. This difference is critical—it determines where tourist interest originates and therefore why income increases occur.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to determine what researchers must concede
steps.content: The study found tourism increases income at both PAs. But the location difference creates a logical problem: Lower Zambezi's tourism income must stem from the PA itself (it's the only major attraction), whereas Abrolhos's income could come from nearby amenities instead (tourists might be drawn to those, not the PA). The researchers concede this distinction—Lower Zambezi's tourism activity is attributable to interest in the PA, while Abrolhos's tourism activity may be unrelated to the PA. The other choices either claim non-tourism sources at Abrolhos (contradicting the study's tourism focus), assert unfounded differences in growth rates or stability, or fail to address the attribution problem. That matches choice C — C is correct.
